approach: Validate the issue's four measurements before scoping; measurement 1 (zero query-corpus files) is false — nine tracked files exist for three subjects, orphaned rather than absent, so that instrument is adopt-and-wire, not build-from-nothing

Route the check on the step-form invariant rather than the reported eligibility-flag class; the flag variant catches one of the five bad outlines, needs the corpus's first spec-to-implementation crossing, and cannot resolve three of twelve subjects, so its marginal delta over the form check measures zero

Record the second outlier the issue missed: one suite carries four outlines that grade deterministic decision tables rather than activation, so the issue's cross-node thesis does not reach them and deleting them as written would destroy sound frozen contracts
Land the form rule in the feature-form authority rather than the node-shape auditor, which would violate that node's documented non-goals
Treat the advisory tier as a prerequisite unit: the authority is binary today, and a blocking rule would force a narrowing this mission has no authority to make
Ablate what lands: revert the rule for a non-zero delta, with the ten canonical outlines as the control that must survive unflagged
Stop at the Clearance floor for deleting or moving outlines and for the re-tag; record the trade and emit a verdict packet rather than self-asserting past it

why: Paused before the spec gate by owner decision: the plugins-as-projects refactor lands first. The check's home is wired into CI by hardcoded paths that audit one project of ten and miss the nested specs entirely; landing the form rule onto that wiring would inherit the broken scope. Not a hard-floor stop — a sequencing choice.

Cold sdd impl-judge round 2 IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true, BLOCKER null; 5/5 frozen scenarios PASS (advisory tier surfaced-not-blocking, the partition invariant, @trigger contract fires, and the two controls: contract-carrying outline and untagged outline both unflagged). Round 1 passed all 5 scenarios but blocked on a structural gap the scenarios could not see: the design docs described the new tier while the operational runbooks still read 'Exit 0 = form clean', so an actor following them reports clean and never relays the advisory lines. Treated as a rule not a site — swept every doc stating check-suite's exit contract and found two MORE beyond the one the judge named, including spec-producer-governance (the actor that authors @trigger outlines, and the only point the free @behavior re-tag is still free). Round 2 ran its own independent sweep for a fourth site and found none. Ablation: 7 mutants, all killed, baseline green in every arm; corpus delta 5->0; control = the 10 canonical outlines unflagged in every arm. Two ablation results recorded: (1) the corpus alone CANNOT discriminate require-both-columns from require-either (both arms report the same 5, since all 5 lack both) — only the unit tests kill those mutants; (2) the drop-isOutline mutant initially SURVIVED because its test's fixture (a plain Scenario with no Examples table) was absorbed by the no-table clause, so the test never reached the guard it was named for. pnpm verify 34/34.

CLEARANCE FLOOR. The additive half is landed, ablated, and impl-gate approved. Everything remaining narrows a frozen scenario, so it is the Council's call and is NOT self-asserted. Three asks in the verdict packet: (1) re-tag the 4 cyberlegion-plugin/dispatch outlines @trigger->@behavior — content-preserving, their Scenario Outline use is sanctioned by suite-format :598 and only the tag is wrong; recommend grant. (2) ssa-lowering's outline: re-tag @behavior (content-preserving) OR adopt the query/should_trigger contract (a narrowing that rewrites its Examples rows); mission does not pick, recommends the former since the latter needs a query corpus this subject does not have. (3) promoting the rule advisory->blocking: NOT yet — it reds main until (1) and (2) land; it belongs to whichever CR closes the last mis-tag. Counter-argument recorded as the brief requires: the negative rows DO encode real design intent, which is why no deletion is asked for here; any future deletion must land that intent in the subject's description prose (the only field the node owns) in the same change. Also recorded: the issue's proposed eligibility-flag mechanism was NOT landed — it is dominated (catches 1 of 5, needs the corpus's first spec->impl crossing, cannot resolve 3 of 12 subjects, marginal delta over the form check measures zero), and the issue's premise 'query-corpus files: zero' is FALSE (nine tracked files, 3 subjects, orphaned not absent).
